title: Privilege catalog
description: Formal shape of privilege-catalog.yaml — the named privileges an agent may be permitted to do (per COR-028). Each entry declares a recognizer (structured matcher over Bash commands and/or harness tool names, with a regex escape hatch) and an optional scope dimension. Namespace owner: permission-grants references entries via the typed token `[privilege-catalog:<id>]` (COR-019). Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

privileges.description: Privilege definitions. Keys are kebab-case ids; values match $defs.entry.

privilege_ref.description: Narrowed cross-schema reference pattern for the privilege-catalog namespace (per COR-019). Consumers $ref this rather than redeclaring the pattern.

bash_matcher.description: One Bash-command recognizer. Either a structured matcher (cmd [+ subcommand] [+ flag_any]) or a regex escape hatch (pattern). The runtime matcher segments compound commands and strips env-var prefixes before matching.

subcommand.description: If present, the first non-flag token after `cmd` must be one of these (e.g. `[run, exec]` to match `docker run` but not `docker ps`).

flag_any.description: If present, the command must carry at least one of these flags (e.g. `[-rf, -fr]` for a destructive-rm guardrail).

pattern.description: Regex escape hatch matched against a command segment when the structured fields can't express the case. The author owns its fragility (Q4c).

description.description: One-line, domain-readable description of what this privilege permits.

recognize.description: How a request is identified as this privilege. At least one of `bash` / `tool`.

bash.description: Bash-command recognizers; a request matches if any item matches a command segment.

tool.description: Harness tool names this privilege covers (e.g. `Read`, `WebFetch`).

scope_type.description: The scope dimension a grant of this privilege may constrain. `directory` = path globs (enforced per-call by the hook); `domain` = host allowlist. Absent = unscoped (anywhere).

guardrail.description: If true, the model synthesizes a baseline `{subject: all, effect: deny}` grant for this privilege — the catalog is the single source of truth for the guardrail deny set (per ADR-002's double-lock). The synthesized model deny is the fail-open hook half; the harness's native settings denies are the fail-closed half. Absent = false.
